body: Do you mean "no" to using the literal keys "X Values" and "Y Values" here? I don't have a strong opinion on that, I was just thinking that since the previous keys were "XY Plot X Type", the "XY Plot" could be taken for granted, but I can see how it might be better to explicitly say "XY Plot X Values", and "XY Plot Fixed X Values", etc. as "X Values" could easily collide with something else in the future when parsing infotext.
author: RnDMonkey

body: I'm thinking it's preferable to strip leading or trailing spaces and slashes here in case some future pattern does return a blank and isn't handled, or if a user just messes up and adds those things to the pattern directly, there doesn't seem to be a downside to some additional stripping here?
author: RnDMonkey

body: I agree with the early comment that having [styles] return "None, None" if styles aren't in use, wasn't very elegant, so I instead made it return nothing. But then that exposed a problem when a commenter used `[prompt_no_styles] + [styles]` as a directory naming pattern. Running strip() on this line wouldn't have prevented the trailing space because it was built into the user's input. Returning "No styles" makes the issue moot (unless they had a trailing space in the style name, I guess?), and I think it is an overall more helpful return value if a user is using [styles] in their naming patterns.
author: RnDMonkey

body: Just added a few comments to simplify the code - this looks great otherwise :)
author: danthe3rd

body: nit: you don't need to pass the `op` argument. If you leave it out, xformers will select the best kernel to dispatch to automatically - this will also simplify the code.
author: danthe3rd

body: xformers can take the inputs in format BNHD, you can skip the rearrange here (and also the rearrange of the output) - that might improve the performance further
author: danthe3rd

body: I added rearrange because of the _maybe_init function. If it could be repurposed for BNHD it'd be great to just pass BNHD.
author: C43H66N12O12S2

body: I actually wrote it without op at first, but it just wouldn't work for some reason. It'd run but return a broken image. I think you had commented on that earlier, broken PR too. No idea why it's needed, but doesn't work without it.
author: C43H66N12O12S2

body: Also, input before rearrange is batch, seq, head * dim, so we'd need to rearrange anyways to split heads, I think.
author: C43H66N12O12S2

body:
> Also, input before rearrange is batch, seq, head * dim, so we'd need to rearrange anyways to split heads, I think.

this is just a change in the strides of the tensor, and wouldn't require any data copy, so that should be fine in terms of performance.

> It'd run but return a broken image

Did you still split the head from the embedding when doing that? If your format is `B N (H*D)` this will be interpreted as `(B*H) N D` by xformers and produce the wrong output.
Could you print the following to help me debug things?
```
print("attention_op", self.attention_op)
print("dispatch", xformers.ops.AttentionOpDispatch.from_arguments(q, k, v))
print("dispatch_op", xformers.ops.AttentionOpDispatch.from_arguments(q, k, v).op)
```
author: danthe3rd

body:
Problem has been resolved, see

https://github.com/AUTOMATIC1111/stable-diffusion-webui/pull/

basically had overlooked doing initialization via xformers.ops.AttentionOpDispatch
author: Thomas-MMJ

body: The thing is that users shouldn't need to specify `op` (unless very specific cases). xformers should figure out the best Operator to dispatch to depending on the inputs.
author: danthe3rd

body:
@danthe3rd 
```
attention_op <class 'xformers.ops.MemoryEfficientAttentionCutlassOp'>
dispatch AttentionOpDispatch(dtype=torch.float16, device=device(type='cuda', index=0), k=40, has_dropout=False, attn_bias_type=<class 'NoneType'>, kv_len=77, q_len=4096, kv=40)
dispatch_op <class 'xformers.ops.MemoryEfficientAttentionCutlassOp'>
```

When I had tested, B * H N D failed, as did B N H D.
author: C43H66N12O12S2

body: @danthe3rd Tagging you so that you don't waste your time trying to debug this. The proper way (without op argument and without BH N * D to B * H ND) works now. The exact same code, actually. No idea why it ever failed or what made it work.

author: Mikubill

body: Does every model require its own copy of `stable-diffusion-v1-5`? Can it be downloaded just once to `models/dreambooth` instead?
author: 0xItx

body: IMO it's a good idea to allow `save_checkpoint` to receive a pipeline (instead of a path on disk) to save on I/O & RAM
author: 0xItx

body:
Technically, *no* model requires a copy. The files are extracted from the target checkpoint when you create a new dreambooth model. They will reside on disk until training is completed and the datafolder for that model is deleted manually.

The only file that actually gets downloaded is the config file needed to load the model.
author: d8ahazard

body:
Good point. I can implement this, just need to decide how to handle the "save_checkpoint" call that runs in "start_training".

It's really not necessary if I update the logic a bit at the "check for save" part to ensure we're saving on the last iteration of training. Maybe I'm already doing this...

                    aesthetic_steps = gr.Slider(minimum=0, maximum=50, step=1, label="Aesthetic steps", value=5)

I believe it's better to decrease the max value, as it is recommended that users try small step sizes. In any case, if they would like to test with more than 50 steps, they could increase the aesthetic_lr parameter to achieve a similar effect

body: 图像上的噪声确实表现为随机彩色噪点。但 latent space 中填入噪声不等于你喂了一张充满rgb噪声的图片。stable diffusion 里面的 latent space 虽然与输入数据有相同的维度（其他模型中可以是更高维的数据），但不等于是图片或者说rgb数据。

author: dtlnor

body:
说一下我的理解，如有错误请指出。
Denoising strength其实也是术语，特指在SD这里。这里的denoise其实并非常规的denoise（不是那种后处理滤镜）。而是同时指“加噪”强度（所以我一开始的翻译“模糊”是错的，那种只适合描述常规降噪滤镜）。在sd进行sampling的过程中，他要先有noise（高斯噪点）然后逐渐从noise“恢复”成图片。这个“恢复”的过程就叫denoise。而假设我们有1份noise（100%），step则决定每个step去除多少%的noise。比如100步就是每步1%。
i2i的生成过程就是，假如你的denoise强度是1.0，那他会加100%的噪点，然后跑100%的sampling。而假如denoise是0.0，那他就不加噪点，也就是不做sampling，直接encode进latent然后再直接decode回来。所以很自然0.5的denoise就只会跑一半的step，因为他只加了50%的noise。
所以其实在sd官方，这个参数不叫Denoising strength，而是直接叫strength（strength for noising/unnoising）
因此其实想靠翻译表达准确的含义是挺难的，我觉得以功能取代原有名字也是一种解决办法。
author: dtlnor

body: extendAPI would need to be called prior to the gradio server starting in webui.py - you should think about how this should hook into extensions at that level (on startup)
author: ArcticFaded

body:
The API is the very last thing to launch, all extensions and scripts are loaded before. Just create your endpoints and call `extendApi` when your script file is loaded and that's all.

For example, in `extensions/test-extension/scripts` create a python file with this code

```python
from fastapi import FastAPI
from modules.api.api import extendApi

subapi = FastAPI()

@subapi.get("/sub")
def read_sub():
    return {"message": "Hello World from sub API"}

extendApi("/subapi", subapi)
```

And that all you need, you can now start the server and go to `localhost:7860/subapi/docs` to see the documentation of your new endpoint.

author: ArcticFaded

body:
There is a override settings field in img2txt and img2img request where you can use specific settings (including using a particular hypernetwork and model) for that inference, then restoring to previous state. Unfortunately, due to long loading times for the model, it doesnt work for model switching, but it is trivial enough to make it load on change. I already have an open PR to enable the hypernetwork loading - though it has not been merged yet. https://github.com/AUTOMATIC1111/stable-diffusion-webui/pull/4120
You can see this PR to see how to add support for reloading model.
author: aliencaocao

Unfortunately, due to long loading times for the model, it doesnt work for model switching, but it is trivial enough to make it load on change. I already have an open PR to enable the hypernetwork loading - though it has not been merged yet. #4120 You can see this PR to see how to add support for reloading model.

I tried overriding `sd_model_checkpoint` with the model `title`, `model_name` and `hash` but none seemed to work as the hash for each image came back with the default model hash.

author: aliencaocao

body:
Hmm, I've been thinking about this and I agree that it's probably best done as part of the generation request itself. Otherwise, a user that tries to generate with another model may end up switching the model for pending generations, unless the client has its own internal queuing mechanism (which other clients won't be able to share.)

Has anyone made a PR like #4120 for switching the model within the request itself?

author: leppie

body:
Good call. Used your key - I'll also add this to my conversion scripts for dreambooth, as that's where this came from.

Also added a bit to ensure we don't load the checkpoint twice if calling from reload_model_weights, a check in the whole checkpoint for the key if the model was created without a "state_dict" entry, and made the log message a bit more useful.

Tested with the v2.0 768-v checkpoint, v2.0 512 checkpoint, several v1x checkpoints, and the new v2.1 checkpoints. All load as intended with no yaml files needed.

Need to find the number of steps for the 512 v2.1 model - but only if the 2.1 512 doesn't use v-prediction.
